front: Work: Rupa Mehra searches for a suitable husband for her daughter Lata in this massive novel
Work: in this novel, Lata rejects the Muslim cricketer Kabir and marries the shoe manufacturer Haresh
Work: this novel's table of contents consists of 19 rhyming couplets
Work: Kabir is disappointed to be cast as Malvolio in a rendition of Twelfth Night in this novel
Work: a riot is caused by the decision to build a Hindu temple near an existing mosque in this novel
Work: a character writes a wedding proposal through the acrostic 'A Modest Proposal' in this novel
Work: a courtesan reveals that Tasneem is her daughter, causing Maan to stab Firoz in jealousy in this novel
Work: the child math prodigy Bhaskar befriends Dr. Durrani in this novel
Work: this verse novel about San Francisco yuppies is written entirely in 590 Onegin stanzas
Work: programmer John Brown meets the lawyer Liz Dorati through a newspaper ad in this verse novel
Work: a character declares 'The Dow-Jones of my heart is depressed' in this verse novel
Work: Janet Hayakawa is a sculptor/drummer for the band Liquid Sheep in this novel
Work: an anti-nuclear protest at Lungless Labs features in this verse novel
Work: the despised cat Charlemagne is sent to 'Psycho-Kitty' after urinating on a pillow in this novel
Work: violinist Michael Holme of the Maggiore Quartet rekindles his romance with pianist Julia McNicholl in this novel
Work: the two central characters of this novel spot each other on opposite buses on Oxford Street in London
Work: the pianist in this novel is married and going deaf
Author: this Indian author's planned sequel A Suitable Girl was delayed by his breakup with violinist Philippe Honore
Work: this nonfiction book follows the author's great-aunt Hennerle Gerda Caro, interspersed with photographs and letters
Work: this poetry collection includes translations of Du Fu ('Drifting, what am I like? A gull between earth and sky') and Wang Wei's 'The Deer Park'
